expressions.english: something to drink; beverage
to drink alcohol
to drink water
What do you want to drink?
I want to drink green tea.
I can't drink alcohol.
I don't drink coffee.
Shall I give you something to drink?
Try drinking this.
Go and buy me something to drink.
expressions.notes: 사다 주다 = to buy something for someone and bring it to that person

expressions.english: I was thirsty, so I drank water.
expressions.notes:
목 = throat; neck
마르다 = to dry; to be dried
목이 마르다 = to be thirsty

expressions.english: Do you like drinking wine?
I drink coffee every morning.
Drinking cola makes me feel happy.
expressions.notes:
콜라 = cola
기분 = feelings; mood
좋다 = to be good, likable; to be desirable; to be nice; to like
-어/아/여지다 = to become + adjective

expressions.english: Today, I drank as many as three cups of coffee.
expressions.notes:
오늘 = today
잔 = counter for glasses, cups, or shots
-이나 = as many as + number, as much as + amount

expressions.english: Last night, I drank too much alcohol.

expressions.korean: 삼겹살을 먹을 때는 소주가 마시고 싶어져요.
expressions.english: When I eat samgyeopsal, I feel like drinking soju.
expressions.notes:
삼겹살 = pork belly meat
소주 = soju

expressions.korean: 너무 추워서 따뜻한 음료수가 마시고 싶어요.
expressions.english: It is really cold, so I want to drink a hot beverage.
expressions.notes:
춥다 = to be cold
따뜻하다 = to be warm
따뜻한 is the adjective form of 따뜻하다.
음료수 = beverage

expressions.korean: 오늘 저녁에 친구하고 맥주 마시러 갈 거예요.
expressions.english: This evening, I am going to go drink beer with my friend.

expressions.korean: 불이 났을 때는 연기를 마시지 않도록 주의해야 해요.
expressions.english: When there's a fire, you have to be careful to not inhale the smoke.
expressions.notes:
불 = fire
불이 나다 = to have a fire; a fire breaks out
연기 = smoke
-지 않도록 = not to + V
주의하다 = to be careful (to/not to + V)
-어/아/여야 하다 = should; have to; must
